content_markdown:
A periodic driving force of frequency $f$ acts on a system which undergoes forced oscillations of amplitude $A$. The graph below shows the variation with $f$ of $A$. The maximum amplitude $A_0$ of the oscillations occurs at frequency $f_0$.

The damping of the system is now increased.

Which describes the change in $f_0$ and the change in $A_0$?

diagram_metadata.description: A resonance curve showing amplitude A on the y-axis and driving frequency f on the x-axis. The peak occurs at frequency f0 with a maximum amplitude A0.

vibe_explanation: When damping is increased in a forced oscillation system, two main things happen to the resonance curve: 1) The peak becomes broader and the maximum amplitude $A_0$ decreases because more energy is dissipated per cycle. 2) The resonant frequency $f_0$ shifts slightly to the left (decreases) as the damping force opposes the motion and effectively slows the response of the system.
